cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure out the answer to this question. The question is asking what you should do when communicating with a pen pal. The options are A, B, C, D. First, I'll read through the passage again to find the relevant information. The passage mentions several things about pen pals, both email and handwritten. Then there's a section about things to remember when communicating with pen pals in the digital age. Let me check that part again. The passage says: "Make sure to share all letters and e-mails with your parents so they know you are safe. If something seems _ , stop writing letters. Never meet a pen pal in person unless a grown-up is with you." So the key points here are sharing with parents, stopping if something seems off, and not meeting without a grown-up. Now looking at the options: A) keep all the pen pal's letters and e-mails in private. But the passage says to share them with parents, so A is the opposite of what's advised. So A is probably incorrect. B) meet him/her with one of your classmates. The passage says not to meet unless a grown-up is with you. A classmate isn't a grown-up, so B is not correct. The passage advises against meeting without a grown-up, so B is wrong. C) be sure you are safe at any time. The passage emphasizes safety by sharing with parents and not meeting without a grown-up. So ensuring safety is a key point here. This seems like the correct answer. D) share whatever you experience with him/her. The passage says to share
